			TECUMSEH VALLEY

[tab]              [ch]C[/ch]               [ch]C[/ch]  [ch]F[/ch]        [ch]C[/ch]
		The name she gave was Caroline[/tab]
[tab]                [ch]F[/ch]       [ch]F[/ch]     [ch]C[/ch]     [ch]C[/ch]
		Daughter of a miner[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]F[/ch]         [ch]F[/ch]           [ch]Em[/ch]          [ch]Am[/ch]
		And her ways were free and it seemed to me[/tab]
[tab]                [ch]G[/ch]           [ch]G[/ch]         [ch]F[/ch]        [ch]F[/ch]      [ch]C[/ch]     [ch]C[/ch]
		The sunshine walked beside her[/tab]

		She came from Spencer across the hill
		She said her Pa had sent her
		'Cause the coal was low and soon the snow
		Would turn the skies to winter

		She said she'd come to look for work
		She was not seeking favor
		For a dime a day and a place to stay
		She turned those hands to labor

		But the times were hard, Lord, and the jobs were few
		All through Tecumseh Valley
		She'd ask around, and a job she found
		Tendin' bar at Gypsy Sally's

		She saved enough to get back home
		And spring replaced the winter
		But her dreams were denied, her Pa had died
		The word come down from Spencer

		So she turned to whorin' out on the streets
		With all the lust inside her
		And it was many a man returned again
		To lay himself beside her

		They found her down beneath the stairs
		That led to Gypsy Sally's
		And in her hand when she died was a note that cried
		"Fare thee well, Tecumseh Valley".

		-instrumental verse-

		-repeat first verse-

Capo on 2nd

[tab]   [ch]G[/ch]                                     [ch]Am[/ch]

[tab]         [ch]G[/ch]                    [ch]C[/ch]               [ch]D[/ch]                    [ch]G[/ch]  [ch]Gsus4[/ch]

[tab]                 [ch]G[/ch]               [ch]C[/ch]               [ch]D[/ch]                     [ch]G[/ch]   [ch]Gsus4[/ch]

[tab][ch]G[/ch]               [ch]C[/ch]                    [ch]Bm[/ch]          [ch]C[/ch]

[tab]           [ch]G[/ch]                   [ch]C[/ch]              [ch]D[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]

[tab]          [ch]G[/ch]      [ch]G/B[/ch]           [ch]C[/ch]

[tab]               [ch]D[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]      [ch]Gsus4[/ch]

[Verse 3]

[tab][ch]G[/ch]                [ch]C[/ch]                         [ch]Bm[/ch]            [ch]C[/ch]

tabs.song: Speed Of The Sound Of Loneliness (ver 2)
tabs.body:
X-POP3-Rcpt: gvaughn@skye2
Date: Wed, 16 Oct 1996 22:11:53 -0700
From: Hakan Sjostrom 
MIME-Version: 1.0
To: cowpie@olga.net
Subject: CRD: SPEED OF THE SOUND OF LONELINESS rec. by Nancy Griffith
X-URL: http://www.roughstock.com/cowpie/

SPEED OF THE SOUND OF LONELINESS
Recorded by Nancy Griffith
Written by John Prine

INSTRUMENTAL ([ch]C[/ch] [ch]F[/ch] [ch]G7[/ch] [ch]C[/ch] twice, first time slowly)

[tab][ch]C[/ch]                                    [ch]F[/ch]
You come home late and you come home early[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                                      [ch]C[/ch]
  You come on big when you're feelin' small[/tab]
[tab]                                          [ch]F[/ch]
You come home straight and you come home curly[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                                 [ch]C[/ch]
  Sometimes you don't come home at all[/tab]


CHORUS:
[tab]                            [ch]F[/ch]
So what in the world's come over you[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                                   [ch]C[/ch]
  And what in heavens name have you done[/tab]
[tab]                                      [ch]F[/ch]
You broken the speed of the sound of loneliness[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                                            [ch]C[/ch]
  You're out there runnin' just to be on the run[/tab]


[tab]                                    [ch]F[/ch]
Well I've got a heart that burns with a fever[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                                      [ch]C[/ch]
  And I've got a worried and a jealous mind[/tab]
[tab]                               [ch]F[/ch]
How can a love that'll last forever[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                       [ch]C[/ch]
  Now get left so far behind[/tab]


CHORUS


INSTRUMENTAL (Same as chorus)


[tab][ch]C[/ch]                                [ch]F[/ch]
It's mighty mean and a dreadful sorrow[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                              [ch]C[/ch]
  It's crossed the evil line today[/tab]
[tab]                         [ch]F[/ch]
How can you ask about tomorrow[/tab]
[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                               [ch]C[/ch]
  When we ain't got one word to say[/tab]


CHORUS (Repeated twice)

[tab][ch]G7[/ch]                                            [ch]C[/ch]
  You're out there runnin' just to be on the run (Repeated twice)[/tab]

INSTRUMENTAL ([ch]C[/ch] [ch]F[/ch] [ch]G7[/ch] [ch]C[/ch])

tabs.song: Thats The Way That The World Goes Round
tabs.body:
This is a version I learned off a live video of John from an Aug 2008 concert.
If you want it to sound exactly like John's version you'll want to fingerpick it and 
throw in the extra notes (mostly G on the high e string and a high E, F and sometimes D),
you'll get where they are if you listen to it.

 Capo 2nd fret for the 2008 live recording - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F9DA-u2EhmA}
 Capo 2nd fret for the 2016 Stephen Colbert recording - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wvY02SSj450}
 Capo 5th fret for the studio recording


[Intro]
[ch]C[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]   [ch]F[/ch]   [ch]F[/ch]
[ch]C[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]   [ch]G[/ch]   [ch]G[/ch]
[ch]C[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]   [ch]F[/ch]   [ch]F[/ch]
[ch]C[/ch]   [ch]G[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]

[Verse 1]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]
I know a guy that's got a lot to lose.[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]F[/ch]
He's a pretty nice fellow but he's kind of confused.[/tab]
[tab]         [ch]C[/ch]
He's got muscles in his head that ain't never been used.[/tab]
[tab]                           [ch]G[/ch]
Thinks he own half of this town.[/tab]

[tab][ch]C[/ch]
Starts drinking heavy, gets a big red nose.[/tab]
[tab][ch]F[/ch]
Beats his old lady with a rubber hose,[/tab]
[tab]        [ch]C[/ch]
then he takes her out to dinner and buys her new clothes.[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]G[/ch]          [ch]C[/ch]
That's the way that the world goes 'round.[/tab]

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]
That's the way that the world goes 'round.[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]F[/ch]
You're up one day and the next you're down.[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]C[/ch]
It's half an inch of water and you think you're gonna drown.[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]G[/ch]          [ch]C[/ch]
That's the way that the world goes 'round.[/tab]

[Verse 2]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]
I was sitting in the bathtub counting my toes,[/tab]
[tab]         [ch]F[/ch]
when the radiator broke, water all froze.[/tab]
[tab]      [ch]C[/ch]
I got stuck in the ice without my clothes,[/tab]
[tab]                       [ch]G[/ch]
naked as the eyes of a clown.[/tab]

[tab][ch]C[/ch]
I was crying ice cubes hoping I'd croak,[/tab]
[tab]         [ch]F[/ch]
when the sun come through the window, the ice all broke.[/tab]
[tab]  [ch]C[/ch]
I stood up and laughed thought it was a joke[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]G[/ch]          [ch]C[/ch]
That's the way that the world goes 'round.[/tab]

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]
That's the way that the world goes 'round.[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]F[/ch]
You're up one day and the next you're down.[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]C[/ch]
It's half an inch of water and you think you're gonna drown.[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]G[/ch]          [ch]C[/ch]
That's the way that the world goes 'round.[/tab]

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]
That's the way that the world goes 'round.[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]F[/ch]
You're up one day and the next you're down.[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]C[/ch]

tabs.song: O Brother Where Art Thou - Down To The River To Pray (ver 4)
tabs.body:
Down to the River to Pray
 
[tab] [ch]E[/ch]    [ch]E6[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]
As I went down in the river to pray,[/tab]
[tab] [ch]B[/ch]       [ch]B6[/ch]  [ch]B[/ch] [ch]E[/ch]    [ch]B6[/ch]    [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]
Studyin' about that good ol' way[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]A[/ch]         [ch]E[/ch]           [ch]E6[/ch]   [ch]E[/ch]
And who shall wear, the starry crown[/tab]
[tab] [ch]B6[/ch]         [ch]A[/ch]           [ch]E[/ch]
Good Lord, show me the way.[/tab]

[tab][ch]B[/ch]    [ch]E[/ch]
O sisters, let's go down[/tab]
[tab] [ch]E[/ch]     [ch]B[/ch]   [ch]A[/ch]            [ch]E[/ch]
Let's go down, come on down,[/tab]
[tab][ch]B[/ch]    [ch]E[/ch]  
O sisters, let's go down,[/tab]
[tab][ch]E[/ch]     [ch]B[/ch]      [ch]A[/ch]        [ch]E[/ch]
Down in the river to pray.[/tab]




As I went down in the river to pray,
Studyin' about that good ol' way
And who shall wear, the roven crown
Good Lord, show me the way.

O brothers, let's go down
Let's go down, come on down,
Come on brothers, let's go down,
Down in the river to pray.

As I went down in the river to pray,
Studyin' about that good ol' way
And who shall wear, the starry crown
Good Lord, show me the way.

O fathers, let's go down,
Let's go down, come on down,
O fathers, let's go down,
Down in the river to pray.

As I went down in the river to pray,
Studyin' about that good ol' way
And who shall wear, the roven crown
Good Lord, show me the way.

O mothers, let's go down,
Come on down, don't you want to go down,
Come on, mothers, let's go down,
Down in the river to pray.

As I went down in the river to pray,
Studyin' about that good ol' way
And who shall wear, the starry crown
Good Lord, show me the way.

O sinners, let's go down,
Let's go down, come on down,
O sinners, let's go down,
Down in the river to pray.

As I went down in the river to pray,
Studyin' about that good ol' way
And who shall wear, the roven crown
Good Lord, show me the way.


This is a great religious song from the movie "O Brother, Where Art Thou?", and I 
hope you guys enjoy playing this song as much as I do.

[Verse 1]
[tab][ch]Am[/ch]                        [ch]G[/ch]
   Hello darkness, my old friend,[/tab]
[tab]                            [ch]Am[/ch]
I've come to talk with you again,[/tab]
[tab]                      [ch]F[/ch]      [ch]C[/ch]
Because a vision softly creeping,[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]F[/ch]       [ch]C[/ch]
Left its seeds while I was sleeping,[/tab]
[tab]        [ch]F[/ch]                             [ch]C[/ch]
And the vision that was planted in my brain[/tab]
[tab]            [ch]C/B[/ch] [ch]Am[/ch]    [ch]C[/ch]
Still remains[/tab]
[tab]           [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]Am[/ch]
Within the sound of silence.[/tab]

[Verse 2]
[tab]                             [ch]G[/ch]
In restless dreams I walked alone[/tab]
[tab]                        [ch]Am[/ch]
Narrow streets of cobblestone,[/tab]
[tab]                    [ch]F[/ch]        [ch]C[/ch]
'neath the halo of a street lamp,[/tab]
[tab]                          [ch]F[/ch]         [ch]C[/ch]
I turned my collar to the cold and damp[/tab]
[tab]        [ch]F[/ch]                                         [ch]C[/ch]
When my eyes were stabbed by the flash of a neon light[/tab]
[tab]                    [ch]C/B[/ch] [ch]Am[/ch]    [ch]C[/ch]
That split the night[/tab]
[tab]                [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]Am[/ch]
And touched the sound of silence.[/tab]

[Verse 3]
[tab]                         [ch]G[/ch]
And in the naked light I saw[/tab]
[tab]                           [ch]Am[/ch]
Ten thousand people, maybe more.[/tab]
[tab]                    [ch]F[/ch]       [ch]C[/ch]
People talking without speaking,[/tab]
[tab]                    [ch]F[/ch]       [ch]C[/ch]
People hearing without listening,[/tab]
[tab]               [ch]F[/ch]                         [ch]C[/ch]
People writing songs that voices never share[/tab]
[tab]               [ch]C/B[/ch] [ch]Am[/ch]    [ch]C[/ch]
And no one dare[/tab]
[tab]            [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]Am[/ch]
Disturb the sound of silence.[/tab]

[Verse 4]
[tab]                         [ch]G[/ch]
Fools said I, you do not know[/tab]
[tab]                      [ch]Am[/ch]
Silence like a cancer grows.[/tab]
[tab]                      [ch]F[/ch]           [ch]C[/ch]
Hear my words that I might teach you,[/tab]
[tab]                     [ch]F[/ch]           [ch]C[/ch]
Take my arms that I might reach you.[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]F[/ch]                           [ch]C[/ch]        [ch]C/B[/ch] [ch]Am[/ch]
But my words like silent raindrops fell,[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]C[/ch]             [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]Am[/ch]
And echoed in the wells of silence[/tab]

[Verse 5]
[tab]                         [ch]G[/ch]
And the people bowed and prayed[/tab]
[tab]                     [ch]Am[/ch]
To the neon god they made.[/tab]
[tab]                          [ch]F[/ch]      [ch]C[/ch]
And the sign flashed out its warning,[/tab]
[tab]                      [ch]F[/ch]      [ch]C[/ch]
In the words that it was forming.[/tab]
[tab]                       [ch]F[/ch]
And the sign said, the words of the prophets[/tab]
[tab]                           [ch]C[/ch]
Are written on the subway walls[/tab]
[tab]                  [ch]C/B[/ch] [ch]Am[/ch]
And tenement halls[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]C[/ch]                [ch]G[/ch]         [ch]Am[/ch]
And whisper'd in the sounds of silence.[/tab]

[Verse]
[tab][ch]Dm7[/ch]      [ch]F[/ch]          [ch]Bb[/ch]     [ch]F[/ch]
Tried to keep you close to me,[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]Bb[/ch]       [ch]F[/ch]        [ch]C[/ch]
But life got in between[/tab]
[tab][ch]Dm7[/ch]       [ch]F[/ch]         [ch]Bb[/ch]      [ch]F[/ch]
Tried to square not being there[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]Bb[/ch]        [ch]F[/ch]                [ch]C[/ch]

[tab][ch]F[/ch]

[tab][ch]F[/ch]
Hold back the river, hold back[/tab]

[Verse]

tabs.song: Over The Rainbow What A Wonderful World (ver 6)
tabs.body:
This is a great all-acoustic song to play, and is not that hard to play if you know most chords.

Capo 5th fret

For strumming, he does a pick strum kind of thing, with a little rhythm to it, so just
to the song and you'll eventually get it. I would also recommend using your thumb and
fingernails to strum, instead of using a plastic pick. It sounds a lot better.

Chords Used:

         EADGBe
[ch]E[/ch]        022100
[ch]Esus4[/ch]    022200
[ch]A[/ch]        x02220
[ch]Asus2[/ch]    X02200
[ch]F#madd4[/ch]  x4420x
[ch]B7[/ch]       x21202
[ch]B7[/ch]/[ch]A[/ch]    X01202

The F#madd4 chord in particular was clearly visible in an Eagles video, as 
were the A-Asus2 and B7-B7/A changes.  There are other ways to play F#madd4
but I think this is the easiest, which is probably why Frey chose to use it.

They definitely play this in the key of E, but on the studio recording from 
Their Greatest Hits (1971-1975) they aren't tuned to concert standard
(A=440hz), or to anything else that makes sense, like a half-step flat.  I
find that if I set my tuner to A=433hz it comes out just right.  The same is
true of Take It Easy, so my best guess is that whatever they tuned to in
the studio was off a bit.  Play this along with the studio recording and
you'll see what I mean.

This is a modification of another entry I found here, but I can't remember
whose it was.  Constructive criticism is most welcome.  Enjoy!

Original key Eb
The first few lines are picking then he goes into the strumming.


[Verse 1]

[tab]     [ch]G[/ch]                          [ch]C[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]
On a warm summer's evenin' on a train bound for nowhere,[/tab]
[tab]  [ch]C[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]                                 [ch]D[/ch]
I met up with the gambler; we were both too tired to sleep.[/tab]
[tab]   [ch]G[/ch]                               [ch]C[/ch]             [ch]G[/ch]
So we took turns a starin' out the window at the darkness[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]C[/ch]           [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]D[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]
'til boredom overtook us, and he began to speak.[/tab]


[Verse 2]

[tab]         [ch]G[/ch]                             [ch]C[/ch]                [ch]G[/ch]
He said, "Son, I've made a life out of readin' people's faces,[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]C[/ch]                  [ch]G[/ch]                                      [ch]D[/ch]
and knowin' what their cards were by the way they held their eyes.[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]G[/ch]                               [ch]C[/ch]                 [ch]G[/ch]
And if you don't mind my sayin', I can see you're out of aces.[/tab]
[tab]      [ch]C[/ch]             [ch]G[/ch]            [ch]D[/ch]             [ch]G[/ch]
For a taste of your whiskey I'll give you some advice."[/tab]


[Verse 3]

[tab]     [ch]G[/ch]                           [ch]C[/ch]                  [ch]G[/ch]
So I handed him my bottle and he drank down my last swallow. [/tab]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]                [ch]G[/ch]                              [ch]D[/ch]
Then he bummed a cigarette and asked me for a light.[/tab]
[tab]        [ch]G[/ch]                                [ch]C[/ch]                 [ch]G[/ch]
And the night got deathly quiet, and his face lost all expression.[/tab]
[tab]                 [ch]C[/ch]                    [ch]G[/ch]             [ch]D[/ch]                [ch]G[/ch]
Said, "If you're gonna play the game, boy, ya gotta learn to play it right.[/tab]


[Chorus]

[tab]           [ch]G[/ch]                     [ch]C[/ch]             [ch]G[/ch]
You got to know when to hold 'em, know when to fold 'em,[/tab]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]            [ch]G[/ch]                         [ch]D[/ch]
know when to walk away and know when to run.[/tab]
[tab]          [ch]G[/ch]                            [ch]C[/ch]              [ch]G[/ch]
You never count your money when you're sittin' at the table.[/tab]
[tab]                 [ch]C[/ch]          [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]D[/ch]                 [ch]G[/ch]
There'll be time enough for countin' when the dealin's done.[/tab]


[Verse 4] (transpose +1 here if you want to play it correctly)

[tab][ch]G[/ch]                             [ch]C[/ch]             [ch]G[/ch]
Ev'ry gambler knows that the secret to survivin'[/tab]
[tab]   [ch]C[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]                             [ch]D[/ch]
is knowin' what to throw away and knowing what to keep.[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]G[/ch]                         [ch]C[/ch]              [ch]G[/ch]
'Cause ev'ry hand's a winner and ev'ry hand's a loser,[/tab]
[tab]        [ch]C[/ch]                 [ch]G[/ch]              [ch]D[/ch]           [ch]G[/ch]
and the best that you can hope for is to die in your sleep."[/tab]


[Verse 5]

[tab]    [ch]G[/ch]                               [ch]C[/ch]                       [ch]G[/ch]
And when he'd finished speakin', he turned back towards the window,[/tab]
[tab][ch]C[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]                         [ch]D[/ch]
crushed out his cigarette and faded off to sleep.[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]G[/ch]                             [ch]C[/ch]                 [ch]G[/ch]
And somewhere in the darkness the gambler, he broke even.[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]C[/ch]            [ch]G[/ch]                [ch]D[/ch]                [ch]G[/ch]
But in his final words I found an ace that I could keep.[/tab]


[Chorus]

PARADISE CITY - Guns N' Roses
Tabbed by: Vinny

Now you'll have to find out by yourself what rhythm do you want to play, for example in
the intro bit you can only strum the chords once...but if you are playing by yourself
like on an acoustic then it may not sound all that impressive...so what I do is play
the chords,
 [ch]G[/ch] [ch]C[/ch] in (D D UDUDUDUD DD) each and then
[tab] [ch]F[/ch] (D D) [ch]C[/ch] (D D) [ch]G[/ch] (D D UDUD) 
adding lil' dead notes before switching to the next chord, and I swear the rythm[/tab]
changes every time I play.
So figure out the pattern you'll enjoy strumming for this song!

Rhythm A :-
Rhythm for the parts before the verses and in the verses...
[ch]G5[/ch] -(DDUD) -Strike Damp Notes- [ch]F5[/ch] -(UDU) [ch]C5[/ch] -(D) [ch]Bb5[/ch] -(D) x3
[ch]C5[/ch]- (DUDUDDUD)
[ch]C5[/ch]-[ch]Bb5[/ch]-[ch]G[/ch]-[ch]F[/ch]

[tab]Intro: Play [ch]G[/ch] (Open) slide your strumming finger/pick from the low E string to the
higher e..going through one string at a time[/tab]
then play [ch]G[/ch] (Barre) C  F  C  G  x2

Play the chorus part with the into rhythm

[Verse]

[tab][ch]G5[/ch]                      [ch]F5[/ch]        [ch]C5[/ch]
Just an urchin livin' under the street[/tab]

Chords in the original key of F. McCartney plays it in G but uses 
a guitar that is tuned down one full step (D-G-C-F-A-d), that's 
why the song sounds in the key of F. Transposed +2 to G (the way 
McCartney played it in the studio and plays it live until today)
the song is much easier to play.

Transcribed by MH from Bob Dylan's (best!) album, "Infidels".
I have written this in the key of C so it can be easily transposed: to
play it in the original key (F#) put a capo on fret 6.
Modifications/discussions welcome.

    respectively.

tabs.song: An Irish Blessing May Love And Laughter Light Your Ways
tabs.body:
[Intro]

[tab]      [ch]G[/ch]                      [ch]D[/ch]

[tab]         [ch]G[/ch]                    [ch]Cadd9[/ch]

[tab][ch]G[/ch]          [ch]D[/ch]   [ch]Cadd9[/ch]

[Chorus]
[tab]     [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]Cadd9[/ch]    [ch]D[/ch]         [ch]Cadd9[/ch]

[tab]     [ch]G[/ch]        [ch]Am[/ch]         [ch]D[/ch]
and warm your heart and home.[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]G[/ch]       [ch]Cadd9[/ch]       [ch]D[/ch]       [ch]Cadd9[/ch]
May good and faithful friends be yours,[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]Bm[/ch]            [ch]D[/ch]
wherever you may roam.[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]Em[/ch]             [ch]Bm7[/ch]
May peace and plenty bless your world[/tab]
[tab]      [ch]C[/ch]               [ch]G[/ch]
with joy that long endures.[/tab]
[tab]        [ch]G[/ch]           [ch]Cadd9[/ch]   [ch]Bm7[/ch]      [ch]C[/ch]
And may all life's passing seasons bring[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]G[/ch]       [ch]D[/ch]       [ch]G[/ch]
the best to you and yours![/tab]
